nodes.label: TradePlan Dataclass
nodes.rationale: Validates at least one stage in __post_init__ to prevent invalid plans from being created

nodes.label: ActiveTrade Dataclass
nodes.rationale: Uses dataclasses.replace() for immutable updates (follow-up in Task 5) to prevent hidden side effects; not frozen=True unlike other domain types because it is mutated through trade lifecycle

nodes.label: IBroker Protocol
nodes.rationale: Protocol-based architecture enables multiple broker backends (Binance live, testnet, paper trading, backtesting); tests can mock broker without network or database

nodes.label: Settings Futures Config Fields
nodes.rationale: Uses BOT_-prefixed env vars via pydantic-settings; binance_futures_testnet defaults to True for safe development; leverage defaults to 5 as reasonable starting point (range 1-125)

nodes.label: BrokerError Exception Class
nodes.rationale: Unified exception wrapper so callers need not handle BinanceAPIException or BinanceOrderException directly; preserves original exception and numeric error code for debugging

nodes.label: SpotBroker Class
nodes.rationale: Refactored from BinanceBroker; uses dependency injection for Client to simplify testing; immutable ActiveTrade updates via dataclasses.replace(); cancels both TP and SL orders on advance_stage to prevent dangling orders

nodes.label: Task 8 Brief: CLI Refactor and Display Extraction
nodes.norm_label: task 8 brief: cli refactor and display extraction

nodes.label: Task 9 Report: Futures CLI Completed (commit e5e64b8, 94 tests)
nodes.norm_label: task 9 report: futures cli completed (commit e5e64b8, 94 tests)

nodes.label: Ports and Adapters Architecture Pattern
nodes.rationale: core/ has no I/O or exchange-specific code; exchanges, cli, mcp are plugins. Key rule: grep -r 'from trading_bot.exchanges' src/trading_bot/core/ returns nothing. Enables swapping broker implementations without touching strategy or risk logic.

nodes.label: Backtest Live Parity North Star Principle
nodes.rationale: The exact same Strategy → Risk → IBroker code runs in backtest, testnet, and live. Only the data source and broker implementation swap. Never fork strategy/risk logic per mode.
nodes.norm_label: backtest live parity north star principle

nodes.label: Database Schema (9 tables with account_id SaaS seam)
nodes.norm_label: database schema (9 tables with account_id saas seam)

nodes.label: OTOCO Order Endpoint (POST /api/v3/orderList/otoco)
nodes.rationale: Atomic: if SL/TP setup would fail the entry is also rejected — no orphaned positions. Single network round trip. No race window between fill and OCO placement.

nodes.label: Futures Trade Execution Flow (3 separate orders)
nodes.norm_label: futures trade execution flow (3 separate orders)

links.source_location: 8 tables: accounts, strategies, runs, orders, fills, positions, equity_snapshots + alembic_version
Architecture: core/ holds pure domain types and IBroker protocol; exchanges/binance/ holds adapters
Migration Path: BinanceBroker renamed to SpotBroker; import alias kept
Architecture pattern: Ports & Adapters — core/ depends on nothing
advance_stage: cancel current SL+TP, place next stage orders, increment current_stage
MCP Server exposes 9 tools over IBroker
BinanceBroker renamed to SpotBroker; import alias kept in client/__init__.py
RESULT: ActiveTrade returned entry_price, sl/tp order ids
Section 5: Multi-Stage Futures Trade Lifecycle flowchart
